grammar.usage: Completed actions in the past with present result, habits up to now
Actions continuing up to now, emphasizing duration
grammar.example: I have been studying English for three hours.

blocks.tableName: S + have / has + past participle (= V3 / V(ed))

rows.subject:
He / She / It
Singular Nouns
rows.example: EX: John has already invited Tony to his birthday party.

rows.subject:
I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: They have never played this game before.

rows.subject:
He / She / It
Singular Nouns
rows.example: EX: He hasn't finished his homework.

rows.subject:
I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: They haven't arrived at the airport yet.

rows.subject:
Has | He / She / It
Singular Nouns
rows.example: EX: Has your mother met our teacher yet?

rows.subject:
Have | I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: Have you ever gone camping before?

bullets:
a) Action begins in the past and continues in the present.
EX: It has rained since late night yesterday.

b) Describe an action that was done before and is still done now.
EX: Mr. Tom has been the principal for three years.

c) An action completed in the past but important is the result in the present.
EX: I've done all my homework.

1. Don't use present perfect to describe an action that happened in the past that should use past simple.
EX: ✗ I have done my homework yesterday.
EX: ✓ I did my homework yesterday.

2. Don't use past simple to describe an action that just happened in the past OR is still important to the present time.
EX: ✗ I finished my homework! Can I watch TV now?
EX: ✓ I've finished my homework! Can I watch TV now?

a) Common adverbs:
- just, recently, lately, already, before, ever, never, yet
- for + time period (EX: for 3 years)
- since + time point (EX: since yesterday)
- so far = until now = up to now = up to the present
- in/ for/ during/ over + the past/ last + time period

b) Position of adverbs:
- already, never, ever, just: after "have/has" and before V3/V(ed).
- already: can also be at the end of the sentence.
- yet: at the end of the sentence, often used in negative and interrogative sentences.
- so far, recently, lately...: can be at the beginning or end of the sentence.

rows.subject:
He / She / It
Singular Nouns
Uncountable Nouns
rows.example: EX: He has been studying hard for his exams.

rows.subject:
I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: The children have been sitting in their bedrooms for half a day.

rows.subject:
He / She / It
Singular Nouns
Uncountable Nouns
rows.example: EX: She hasn't been practicing for her concert.

rows.subject:
I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: They haven't been planning for the BBQ.

rows.subject:
Has | He / She / It
Singular Nouns
rows.example: EX: Has he been practicing so hard for this competition?

rows.subject:
Have | I / You / We / They
Plural Nouns
rows.example: EX: Have you been reading recently?

bullets:
a) Express an action that continues up to the present time or has just ended.
EX: You've been writing this email for an hour. How long is it going to take you?
b) Common signal words: for, since, just...

blocks.content: 3. PRESENT PERFECT SIMPLE vs. PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS

bullets: - Present Perfect Simple: Emphasize the result of an action (the action has ended and left a result).
- Present Perfect Continuous: Emphasize the process/continuity of a continuous action (the action is temporary, still continuing or continuing).

vocabulary.example: My grandfather has a collection of old long-playing records.
She threaded the needle to sew the button.

vocabulary.meaning: making a lot of loud sounds
vocabulary.example: The classroom was very noisy during the break.

vocabulary.meaning: act of using physical force against someone
vocabulary.example: The two boys got into a fight after school.

vocabulary.meaning: game where players hit a ball over a net
vocabulary.example: We played volleyball on the beach yesterday.

vocabulary.meaning: person who travels over snow on skis
vocabulary.example: The professional skier slided down the mountain.
He reads the morning newspaper with his coffee.
The teacher emphasized the importance of regular practice.

vocabulary.meaning: make a place clean and neat
vocabulary.example: You should tidy your bedroom before going out.
My bedroom is on the second floor.

vocabulary.meaning: ask someone questions for a job or news
vocabulary.example: They will interview several candidates tomorrow.
I heard a strange noise in the kitchen.
